steps.title: Identify when a system has no solution
steps.content: A system of linear equations has no solution when the two lines are parallel. This happens when both equations represent lines with the same slope but different y-intercepts.

steps.title: Check each choice for parallel lines
steps.content:
Examine the slope and y-intercept of each choice:
  • A: $x = 3$ (vertical) and $y = 5$ (horizontal) intersect at (3, 5), so this system has a solution.
  • B: $y = 6x + 6$ has slope 6, while $y = 5x + 6$ has slope 5. Different slopes mean they intersect at one point.
  • C: $y = 16x + 3$ has slope 16 and y-intercept 3; $y = 16x + 19$ has slope 16 and y-intercept 19. Both lines have the same slope but different y-intercepts, making them parallel with no intersection points.
  • D: $y = 5$ is horizontal (slope 0), while $y = 5x + 5$ has slope 5. Different slopes mean they intersect.
Only choice C represents parallel lines. That matches choice C — C is correct.
